text.NRSV: The beginning of the good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
text.Rotherham: Beginning of the glad-message of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: As it is written in the prophet Isaiah, “See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way;
text.Rotherham: According as it is written in Isaiah the prophet—Lo! I send my messenger before thy face, Who shall prepare thy way,—

text.NRSV: the voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,’”
text.Rotherham: A voice of one crying aloud—In the wilderness, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths.

text.NRSV: John the baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: John the Immerser came, in the wilderness, proclaiming an immersion of repentance for remission of sins;

text.NRSV: And people from the whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
text.Rotherham: and there were going out unto him all the Judaea country and all they of Jerusalem, and were being immersed by him in the Jordan river, openly confessing their sins;

text.NRSV: Now John was clothed with camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
text.Rotherham: and John was clothed with camel’s-hair and a leathern girdle about his loins, and was eating locusts and wild honey;

text.NRSV: He proclaimed, “The one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals.
text.Rotherham: and he proclaimed, saying—He that is mightier than I cometh after [me], of whom I am not worthy to stoop and unloose the strap of his sandals:

text.NRSV: I have baptized you with water; but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
text.Rotherham: I, have immersed you, with water,—He, will immerse you, with the Holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was immersed into the Jordan by John;

text.NRSV: And just as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens torn apart and the Spirit descending like a dove on him.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens rending asunder, and, the Spirit, as a dove, descending unto him;

text.NRSV: And a voice came from heaven, “You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and a voice [came] out of the heavens—Thou, art my Son, the Beloved,—In thee, I delight.

text.NRSV: And the Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, the Spirit urgeth him forth into the wilderness;

text.NRSV: He was in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan; and he was with the wild beasts; and the angels waited on him.
text.Rotherham: and he was in the wilderness, forty days, tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts,—and, the messengers, were ministering unto him.

text.NRSV: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God,
text.Rotherham: And after John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee, proclaiming the glad-message of God,

text.NRSV: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near; repent, and believe in the good news.”
text.Rotherham: [and saying]—The season is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God hath drawn near,—Repent ye, and have faith in the glad-message.

text.NRSV: As Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the sea—for they were fishermen.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by near the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon, and Andrew the brother of Simon, casting a net in the sea,—for they were fishers;

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fish for people.”
text.Rotherham: and Jesus said unto them—Come after me, and I will make you become fishers of men;

text.NRSV: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, leaving the nets, they followed him.

text.NRSV: As he went a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John, who were in their boat mending the nets.
text.Rotherham: And, going forward a little, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, and them who were in the boat putting in order the nets;

text.NRSV: Immediately he called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and followed him.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, he called them,—and, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat, with the hired men, they came away after him.

text.NRSV: They went to Capernaum; and when the sabbath came, he entered the synagogue and taught.
text.Rotherham: And they journey into Capernaum. And, straightway, on the Sabbath, entering into the synagogue, he began teaching;

text.NRSV: They were astounded at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
text.Rotherham: and they were being struck with astonishment at his teaching,—for he was teaching them as one having authority, and, not as the Scribes.

text.NRSV: Just then there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit,
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, there was in their synagogue, a man in an impure spirit,—and he cried out aloud,

text.NRSV: and he cried out, “What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: saying—What have we in common with thee, Jesus of Nazareth? Hast thou come to destroy us? I know thee, who thou art, The Holy One of God.

text.NRSV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus rebuked him, [saying]—Be silenced, and come forth out of him!

text.NRSV: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.
text.Rotherham: And the impure spirit, tearing him, and calling out with a loud voice, came forth out of him;

text.NRSV: They were all amazed, and they kept on asking one another, “What is this? A new teaching—with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
text.Rotherham: and they were amazed, one and all, so that they began to discuss among themselves, saying—What is this? New teaching! With authority, to the impure spirits also, he giveth orders, and they obey him!

text.NRSV: At once his fame began to spread throughout the surrounding region of Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the report of him, straightway, on every hand, into the surrounding country of Galilee.

text.NRSV: As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, out of the synagogue, going forth, he went unto the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

text.NRSV: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.
text.Rotherham: Now, the mother-in-law of Simon, was lying in a fever, and, straightway, they speak to him concerning her;

text.NRSV: He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up. Then the fever left her, and she began to serve them.
text.Rotherham: and, coming near, he raised her up, grasping her hand,—and the fever left her, and she began ministering unto them.

text.NRSV: That evening, at sunset, they brought to him all who were sick or possessed with demons.
text.Rotherham: And, evening arriving, when the sun went in, they were bearing unto him all who were sick, and them who were demonized;

text.NRSV: And the whole city was gathered around the door.
text.Rotherham: and all the city was gathered unto the door;

text.NRSV: And he cured many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
text.Rotherham: and he cured many that were sick with divers diseases, and, many demons, he cast out, and suffered not the demons to be talking,—because they knew him [to be Christ].

text.NRSV: In the morning, while it was still very dark, he got up and went out to a deserted place, and there he prayed.
text.Rotherham: And, very early by night arising, he went out [and departed] into a desert place, and, there, was praying;

text.NRSV: And Simon and his companions hunted for him.
text.Rotherham: and Simon, and they who were with him, went in quest of him,

text.NRSV: When they found him, they said to him, “Everyone is searching for you.”
text.Rotherham: and found him, and say unto him—All, are seeking thee;

text.NRSV: He answered, “Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim the message there also; for that is what I came out to do.”
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Let us be going elsewhere, into the neighbouring country-towns, in order that, there also, I may be making proclamation,—for, to this end, came I forth;

text.NRSV: And he went throughout Galilee, proclaiming the message in their synagogues and casting out demons.
text.Rotherham: and he came, making proclamation, into their synagogues, throughout the whole of Galilee,—and, was casting the demons out.

text.NRSV: A leper came to him begging him, and kneeling he said to him, “If you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And there cometh unto him, a leper, beseeching him and [kneeling],—saying unto him—If thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me;

text.NRSV: Moved with pity, Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, “I do choose. Be made clean!”
text.Rotherham: and, moved with compassion, he stretched forth the hand, and touched him, and saith unto him—I am willing, Be cleansed!

text.NRSV: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
text.Rotherham: and, straightway, the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed;

text.NRSV: After sternly warning him he sent him away at once,
text.Rotherham: and, strictly charging him, straightway, he urged him forth;

text.NRSV: saying to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: and saith unto him—Mind! unto no one, say aught,—but withdraw, thyself, show unto the priest, and offer for thy cleansing what things Moses enjoined, for a witness unto them.

text.NRSV: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer go into a town openly, but stayed out in deserted places; and people came to him from every quarter.
text.Rotherham: But, he, going forth, began to be proclaiming many things, and blazing abroad the story, so that, no longer, was it possible for him, openly, into a city, to enter,—but, outside, in desert places, [was he], and they were coming unto him from every quarter.

text.NRSV: When he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
text.Rotherham: And, entering again into Capernaum, after some days, it was heard say—He is in a house;

text.NRSV: So many gathered around that there was no longer room for them, not even in front of the door; and he was speaking the word to them.
text.Rotherham: and many were gathered together, so that no longer was there room even in the approaches to the door,—and he began speaking unto them the word;

text.NRSV: Then some people came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.
text.Rotherham: and they come, bearing unto him a paralytic, upborne by four,—

text.NRSV: And when they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him; and after having dug through it, they let down the mat on which the paralytic lay.
text.Rotherham: and, not being able to get near him by reason of the multitude, they uncovered the roof where he was, and, having broken it up, they began letting down the couch whereon the paralytic was lying;

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: and, Jesus, seeing their faith, saith unto the paralytic—Child! forgiven are thy sins!

text.NRSV: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
text.Rotherham: Now there were certain of the Scribes there, sitting and deliberating in their hearts,—

text.NRSV: “Why does this fellow speak in this way? It is blasphemy! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
text.Rotherham: Why doth, this, man thus talk? he is speaking profanely! Who can forgive sins, save one, God?

text.NRSV: At once Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were discussing these questions among themselves; and he said to them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, straightway, taking note in his spirit, that [thus] they are deliberating within themselves, saith [unto them]—Why, these things, are ye deliberating in your hearts?

text.NRSV: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and take your mat and walk’?
text.Rotherham: Which is easier—to say unto the paralytic, Forgiven are thy sins, or to say, Rise, [and] take up thy couch, and be walking?

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know that the Son of Man hath authority to be forgiving sins upon the earth, he saith to the paralytic:

text.NRSV: “I say to you, stand up, take your mat and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: To thee, I say, Rise, take up thy couch, and be going thy way unto thy house.

text.NRSV: And he stood up, and immediately took the mat and went out before all of them; so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
text.Rotherham: And he arose, and, straightway taking up the couch, went forth before all,—so that all were beside themselves and were glorifying God, [saying]—Thus, we never saw it!

text.NRSV: Jesus went out again beside the sea; the whole crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text.Rotherham: And he went forth again by the sea,—and all the multitude was coming unto him, and he began teaching them.

text.NRSV: As he was walking along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he got up and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, passing by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting over the tax-office,—and saith unto him—Be following me! And, arising, he followed him.

text.NRSV: And as he sat at dinner in Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were also sitting with Jesus and his disciples—for there were many who followed him.
text.Rotherham: And it cometh to pass, that he is reclining in his house, and many tax-collectors and sinners were reclining with Jesus and his disciples; for there were many, and they began following him.

text.NRSV: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and Pharisees, seeing that he was eating with the sinners and the tax-collectors, began saying unto his disciples—With the tax-collectors and sinners, is he eating?

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he said to them, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I have come to call not the righteous but sinners.”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing it, Jesus saith unto them—No need, have the strong of a physician, but they who are sick, I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.

text.NRSV: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and people came and said to him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting; and they come and say unto him—For what cause, do, the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees, fast, whereas, thy disciples, fast not?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—Is it possible for the sons of the bridechamber, while the bridegroom is with them, to be fasting? So long as they have the bridegroom with them, it is impossible, to fast.

text.NRSV: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
text.Rotherham: But there will come days, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and, then, they will fast, in that day.

text.NRSV: “No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.
text.Rotherham: No one, seweth a patch of unshrunk cloth upon an old mantle,—otherwise, at least, the shrinking teareth away from it—the new from the old—and, a worse rent is made.

text.NRSV: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins; but one puts new wine into fresh wineskins.”
text.Rotherham: And, no one, poureth new wine into fold skins,—otherwise, at least, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and, the skins. [But new wine is for unused skins.]

text.NRSV: One sabbath he was going through the grainfields; and as they made their way his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that he, on the Sabbath, was passing through the cornfields, and his disciples began to be going forward, plucking the ears of corn.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, were saying,—See! why are they doing, on the Sabbath, what is not allowed?

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need of food?
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto them—Have ye never read what David did, when he had need and hungered,—he, and they who were with him:

text.NRSV: How he entered the house of God, when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and he gave some to his companions?”
text.Rotherham: [how] he entered into the house of God, while Abiathar was High-priest, and, the presence-bread, did eat,—which it is not allowed to eat, save unto the priests,—and gave, unto them also who were with him?

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The sabbath was made for humankind, and not humankind for the sabbath;
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—The Sabbath, for man, was made, and not, man, for, the Sabbath:

text.NRSV: so the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: So that the Son of Man is, Lord, even of the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
text.Rotherham: And he entered again into a synagogue, and there-was there, a man having, his hand, withered;

text.NRSV: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
text.Rotherham: and they were narrowly watching him, whether, on the Sabbath, he would cure him, that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come forward.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto the man who hath his hand withered, Arise into the midst!

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath, or to do harm, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
text.Rotherham: and saith unto them—Is it allowed, on the Sabbath, to do good, or to do evil? To, save, life, or, to slay? but they remained silent.

text.NRSV: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
text.Rotherham: And, looking round upon them with anger, being at the same time grieved on account of the hardening of their heart, he saith unto the man—Stretch forth thy hand! and he stretched it forth, and his hand, was restored.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisees, going out straightway with the Herodians, were giving counsel against him, that they should, destroy, him.

text.NRSV: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, with his disciples, retired unto the sea; and, a great throng from Galilee, followed, also from Judaea,

text.NRSV: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Zidon, a great throng, hearing whatsoever things he was doing, came unto him.

text.NRSV: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto his disciples that a little boat might attend him, because of the multitude,—that they might not be pressing upon him.

text.NRSV: for he had cured so many that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: For he cured many, so that they were besieging him, that they might touch him—as many as had plagues;

text.NRSV: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, “You are the Son of God!”
text.Rotherham: and the impure spirits, as soon as they beheld him, were falling down to him, and crying aloud, while he was speaking, Thou, art the Son of God!

text.NRSV: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.
text.Rotherham: and sternly was he rebuking them, lest they should make him manifest.

text.NRSV: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
text.Rotherham: And he goeth up into the mountain and calleth near whom, he, pleased,—and they went away unto him;

text.NRSV: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,
text.Rotherham: and he appointed a twelve, whom also he named, Apostles,—that they might be with him, and that he might be sending them forth to make proclamation,

text.NRSV: and to have authority to cast out demons.
text.Rotherham: and to have authority to cast out the demons;

text.NRSV: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
text.Rotherham: and he appointed the twelve (and imposed a name on Simon—) Peter,

text.NRSV: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);
text.Rotherham: and James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James (and imposed on them a name—Boanerges, that is, sons of thunder);

text.NRSV: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,
text.Rotherham: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the zealot;

text.NRSV: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
text.Rotherham: and Judas Iscariot, who also delivered him up. And he cometh into a house;

text.NRSV: Then he went home; and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.
text.Rotherham: and the multitude come together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

text.NRSV: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, “He has gone out of his mind.”
text.Rotherham: And they who were near to him, hearing of it, went out to secure him,—for they were saying—He is beside himself!

text.NRSV: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons.”
text.Rotherham: And, the Scribes who from Jerusalem had come down, were saying—He hath, Beelzebul; and—In the ruler of the demons, casteth he out the demons.

text.NRSV: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?
text.Rotherham: And, calling them near, in parables, began he to say unto them—How can, Satan, cast, Satan out?

text.NRSV: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a kingdom against itself be divided, that kingdom cannot be made to stand;

text.NRSV: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
text.Rotherham: And, if a house against itself be divided, the house shall be unable to stand;

text.NRSV: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.
text.Rotherham: And, if, Satan, hath risen up against himself, and become divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.

text.NRSV: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.
text.Rotherham: But, no one is able into the house of the mighty one to enter, and, his spoils, to carry off, unless, first the mighty one, he bind, and, then his house, will he plunder!

text.NRSV: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—All things shall be forgiven unto the sons of men,—the sins and the profanities wherewithal they shall profane;

text.NRSV: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin”—
text.Rotherham: But, whosoever shall revile against the Holy Spirit, hath no forgiveness, unto times age-abiding,—but is guilty of an age-abiding sin:

text.NRSV: for they had said, “He has an unclean spirit.”
text.Rotherham: because they were saying—An impure spirit, he hath!

text.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.
text.Rotherham: And there come his mother and his brethren, and, standing, outside, they sent unto him, calling him.

text.NRSV: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you.”
text.Rotherham: And there was sitting around him a multitude, and they say unto him—Lo! thy mother and thy brethren, outside, are seeking thee!

text.NRSV: And he replied, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
text.Rotherham: And, answering them, he saith—Who are my mother and my brethren?

text.NRSV: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
text.Rotherham: And, looking around on them who, about him, in a circle, were sitting, he saith, See! my mother and my brethren!

text.NRSV: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
text.Rotherham: Whosoever shall do the will of God, the same, is my brother and sister and mother.

text.NRSV: Again he began to teach beside the sea. Such a very large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the sea and sat there, while the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, again, began he to be teaching by the sea; and there come together unto him a very great multitude, so that, he, into a boat, entering, was sitting upon the sea,—and, all the multitude, were, near the sea upon the land.

text.NRSV: He began to teach them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
text.Rotherham: And he began to teach them, in parables, many things, and was saying unto them, in his teaching—

text.NRSV: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
text.Rotherham: Hearken! Lo! the sower went forth to sow;

text.NRSV: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell by the pathway, and the birds came, and devoured it;

text.NRSV: Other seed fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil, and it sprang up quickly, since it had no depth of soil.

text.NRSV: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they hurried there on foot from all the towns and arrived ahead of them.
text.Rotherham: And many saw them going away, and took note of it, and, afoot, from all the cities, ran they together thither,—and outwent them.

text.NRSV: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and he began to teach them many things.
text.Rotherham: And, coming forth, he saw, a great multitude, and was moved with compassion towards them, because they were like sheep having no shepherd, and he began to be teaching them many things.

text.NRSV: When it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, “This is a deserted place, and the hour is now very late;
text.Rotherham: And, already, a late hour, having arrived, his disciples came unto him and were saying, A desert, is the place, and it is, already, a late hour:

text.NRSV: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding country and villages and buy something for themselves to eat.”
text.Rotherham: dismiss them, that, departing into the surrounding hamlets and villages, they may buy themselves something to eat.

text.NRSV: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They said to him, “Are we to go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give it to them to eat?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said to them—Ye, give them, to eat. And they say unto him—Shall we depart and buy two hundred denaries’ worth of loaves, and give them to eat?

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “How many loaves have you? Go and see.” When they had found out, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, saith unto them—How many loaves, have ye? Go, see! And, getting to know, they say—Five, and, two fishes.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered them to get all the people to sit down in groups on the green grass.
text.Rotherham: And he gave them orders, that all should be made recline, in parties, upon, the green grass.

text.NRSV: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.
text.Rotherham: And they fell back, in companies, by hundreds and by fifties.

text.NRSV: Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people; and he divided the two fish among them all.
text.Rotherham: And, taking the five loaves, and the two fishes, looking up into heaven, he blessed, and brake up the loaves, and began giving unto the disciples, that they might set before them, and, the two fishes, divided he, unto all.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled;
text.Rotherham: And they did all eat, and were filled.

text.NRSV: and they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.
text.Rotherham: And they took up broken pieces, twelve baskets, full measure, and from the fishes.

text.NRSV: Those who had eaten the loaves numbered five thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And they who did eat the loaves were, five thousand men.

text.NRSV: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, constrained he his disciples to enter into the boat, and be going forward to the other side, unto Bethsaida,—while, he, was dismissing the multitude.

text.NRSV: After saying farewell to them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
text.Rotherham: And, bidding them farewell, he departed into the mountain to pray.

text.NRSV: When evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.
text.Rotherham: And, when, evening, came, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and, he alone, on the land.

text.NRSV: When he saw that they were straining at the oars against an adverse wind, he came towards them early in the morning, walking on the sea. He intended to pass them by.
text.Rotherham: And, seeing them distressed in the rowing, for the wind was against them, about the fourth watch of the night, he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea,—and was wishing to pass by them.

text.NRSV: But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out;
text.Rotherham: But, they, seeing him, upon the sea, walking, supposed that it was an apparition, and cried out aloud;

text.NRSV: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said, “Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.”
text.Rotherham: for, all, saw him, and were troubled. But, he, straightway, talked with them, and saith unto them—Take courage! it is, I—be not afraid!

text.NRSV: Then he got into the boat with them and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
text.Rotherham: And he went up unto them, into the boat,—and the wind lulled. And, exceedingly, within themselves, were they astonished;

text.NRSV: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
text.Rotherham: for they understood not by the loaves,—but their, heart, had been, hardened.

text.NRSV: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored the boat.
text.Rotherham: And crossing over unto the land, they came unto Gennesaret, and cast anchor near.

text.NRSV: When they got out of the boat, people at once recognized him,
text.Rotherham: And, when they had, come forth, out of the boat, straightway, recognizing him,

text.NRSV: and hurried about that whole region and began to bring the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
text.Rotherham: the people ran round the whole of that country, and began to be carrying round, upon couches, them who were sick, wherever they heard that he was.

text.NRSV: And wherever he went, into villages or cities or farms, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.
text.Rotherham: And, wheresoever he was entering into villages, or into cities, or into hamlets, in the marketplaces, laid they the sick, and were beseeching him, that, only the fringe of his mantle, they might touch, and, as many soever as touched it, were being made well.

verses.text: Now when the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,
they noticed that some of his disciples were eating with defiled hands, that is, without washing them.
(For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, keeping the tradition of the elders;
and they do not eat anything from the market unless they wash it; and there are also many other traditions that they observe, the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles.)
So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
He said to them, “Isaiah prophesied rightly about you hypocrites, as it is written, ‘This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;
in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.’
You abandon the commandment of God and hold to human tradition.”
Then he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition!
For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.’
But you say that if anyone tells father or mother, ‘Whatever support you might have had from me is Corban’ (that is, an offering to God)—
then you no longer permit doing anything for a father or mother,
thus making void the word of God through your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many things like this.”
Then he called the crowd again and said to them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand:
there is nothing outside a person that by going in can defile, but the things that come out are what defile.”
When he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.
He said to them, “Then do you also fail to understand? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile,
since it enters, not the heart but the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
And he said, “It is what comes out of a person that defiles.
For it is from within, from the human heart, that evil intentions come: fornication, theft, murder,
adultery, avarice, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, envy, slander, pride, folly.
All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
From there he set out and went away to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. Yet he could not escape notice,
but a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him, and she came and bowed down at his feet.
Now the woman was a Gentile, of Syrophoenician origin. She begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not fair to take the children’s food and throw it to the dogs.”
But she answered him, “Sir, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
Then he said to her, “For saying that, you may go—the demon has left your daughter.”
So she went home, found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
Then he returned from the region of Tyre, and went by way of Sidon towards the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
They brought to him a deaf man who had an impediment in his speech; and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
He took him aside in private, away from the crowd, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat and touched his tongue.
Then looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
And immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
Then Jesus ordered them to tell no one; but the more he ordered them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
They were astounded beyond measure, saying, “He has done everything well; he even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak.”

text.NRSV: In those days when there was again a great crowd without anything to eat, he called his disciples and said to them,
text.Rotherham: In those days, there being, again a large multitude, and they not having any thing to eat, calling near the disciples, he saith unto them—

text.NRSV: “I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.
text.Rotherham: I am moved with compassion towards the multitude, because, already three days, abide they with me, and they have nothing to eat;

text.NRSV: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they will faint on the way—and some of them have come from a great distance.”
text.Rotherham: and, if dismiss them fasting unto their home, they will be exhausted in the way,—and, certain of them, are, from afar.

text.NRSV: His disciples replied, “How can one feed these people with bread here in the desert?”
text.Rotherham: And his disciples answered him—Whence shall any one be able, here, to fill, these, with bread, in a desert?

text.NRSV: He asked them, “How many loaves have you?” They said, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: And he was questioning them—How many loaves have ye? and they said—Seven.

text.NRSV: Then he ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground; and he took the seven loaves, and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they distributed them to the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And he sendeth word to the multitude, to fall back upon the ground. And, taking the seven loaves, giving thanks, he brake, and was giving unto his disciples, that they might be setting before them; and they set before the multitude.

text.NRSV: They had also a few small fish; and after blessing them, he ordered that these too should be distributed.
text.Rotherham: And they had a few small fishes; and, blessing them, he bade them set, these also, before them.

text.NRSV: They ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
text.Rotherham: And they did eat, and were filled, and took up remainders of broken pieces, seven hampers.

text.NRSV: Now there were about four thousand people. And he sent them away.
text.Rotherham: Now they were about four thousand; and he dismissed them.

text.NRSV: And immediately he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
text.Rotherham: And, straightway, entering into the boat with his disciples, he came into the parts of Dalmanutha;

text.NRSV: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, asking him for a sign from heaven, to test him.
text.Rotherham: and forth came the Pharisees, and began discussing with him, seeking of him, a sign from the heaven, testing him.

text.NRSV: And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said, “Why does this generation ask for a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
text.Rotherham: And, deeply sighing in his spirit, he saith—Why, doth this generation seek, a sign? Verily, I say—there shall, not be given, to this generation, a sign.

text.NRSV: And he left them, and getting into the boat again, he went across to the other side.
text.Rotherham: And leaving them, again, embarking, he departed unto the other side.

text.NRSV: Now the disciples had forgotten to bring any bread; and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text.Rotherham: And they forgot to take loaves, and, save one loaf, they had nothing with them in the boat.

text.NRSV: And he cautioned them, saying, “Watch out—beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.”
text.Rotherham: And he began charging them, saying—Mind! beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod!

text.NRSV: They said to one another, “It is because we have no bread.”
text.Rotherham: And they began deliberating one with another, because they had no loaves.

text.NRSV: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said to them, “Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not perceive or understand? Are your hearts hardened?
text.Rotherham: And, taking note, he saith unto them—Why do ye deliberate, because ye have no, loaves? Not yet perceive ye, neither understand,—keep ye your hearts, hardened?

text.NRSV: Do you have eyes, and fail to see? Do you have ears, and fail to hear? And do you not remember?
text.Rotherham: Eyes having, see ye not? and ears having, hear ye not? and remember ye not

text.NRSV: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” They said to him, “Twelve.”
text.Rotherham: when, the five loaves, I brake unto the five thousand, how many baskets, full of broken pieces, ye took up? They say unto him—Twelve,—

text.NRSV: “And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?” And they said to him, “Seven.”
text.Rotherham: When the seven, unto the four thousand, how many hampers, full measure, of broken pieces, ye took up? And they say unto him—Seven. And he was saying unto them—

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Do you not yet understand?”
text.Rotherham: Not yet, do ye understand?

text.NRSV: They came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch him.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Bethsaida. And they bring unto him one blind, and beseech him that him, he would touch.

text.NRSV: He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village; and when he had put saliva on his eyes and laid his hands on him, he asked him, “Can you see anything?”
text.Rotherham: And, laying hold of the hand of the blind man, he brought him forth outside the village, and, spitting into his eyes, laying his hands upon him, he was asking him—Anything, seest thou?

text.NRSV: And the man looked up and said, “I can see people, but they look like trees, walking.”
text.Rotherham: and, looking up, he was saying—I see men, because, like trees, I behold them walking

text.Rotherham: Then again, put he his hands upon his eyes, and he saw clearly, and was restored, and was seeing distinctly, in broad splendour, all things together.

text.NRSV: Then he sent him away to his home, saying, “Do not even go into the village.”
text.Rotherham: And he sent him away unto his house, saying—Not even into the village, mayest thou enter.

text.Rotherham: And forth went Jesus and his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea of Philip. And, in the way, he was questioning his disciples, saying unto them—Who, are men saying that I am?

text.NRSV: And they answered him, “John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.”
text.Rotherham: And they answered him, saying—John the Immerser, and, others, Elijah,—and, others, One of the prophets.

text.NRSV: He asked them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered him, “You are the Messiah.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, went on to question them—But, who, say, ye, that I am? Peter, answering, saith unto him—Thou, art, the Christ.

text.NRSV: And he sternly ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
text.Rotherham: And he straitly charged them, that, no one, they should tell concerning him.

text.NRSV: Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man must undergo great suffering, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be teaching them—The Son of Man, must needs suffer many things, and be rejected by the Elders and the High-priests and the Scribes,—and be slain; and, after three days, arise.

text.NRSV: He said all this quite openly. And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
text.Rotherham: And, openly, was he speaking the word. And Peter, taking him aside, began to rebuke him.

text.NRSV: But turning and looking at his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, turning round and looking upon his disciples, rebuked Peter, and saith—Withdraw behind me, Satan! because thou art not regarding the things, of God, but the things, of men.

text.Rotherham: And, calling near the multitude with his disciples, he said unto them—If anyone willeth, after me, to come, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and be following me.

text.Rotherham: For, what doth it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and be made to forfeit his life?

text.NRSV: Indeed, what can they give in return for their life?
text.Rotherham: For what can a man, give, in exchange for his life?

text.NRSV: Those who are ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of them the Son of Man will also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.”
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also, will be ashamed, of him, whensoever he shall come, in the glory of his Father, with the holy messengers.

verses.text: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see that the kingdom of God has come with power.”
Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain apart, by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,
and his clothes became dazzling white, such as no one on earth could bleach them.
And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, who were talking with Jesus.
Then Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; let us make three dwellings, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
He did not know what to say, for they were terrified.
Then a cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud there came a voice, “This is my Son, the Beloved; listen to him!”
Suddenly when they looked around, they saw no one with them any more, but only Jesus.
As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one about what they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what this rising from the dead could mean.
Then they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
He said to them, “Elijah is indeed coming first to restore all things. How then is it written about the Son of Man, that he is to go through many sufferings and be treated with contempt?
But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written of him.”
When they came to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them, and some scribes arguing with them.
When the whole crowd saw him, they were immediately overcome with awe, and they ran forward to greet him.
He asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?”
Someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought you my son; he has a spirit that makes him unable to speak;
and whenever it seizes him, it dashes him down; and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid; and I asked your disciples to cast it out, but they could not do so.”
He answered them, “You faithless generation, how much longer must I be among you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him to me.”
And they brought the boy to him. When the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth.
Jesus asked the father, “How long has this been happening to him?” And he said, “From childhood.
It has often cast him into the fire and into the water, to destroy him; but if you are able to do anything, have pity on us and help us.”
Jesus said to him, “‘If you are able!’—All things can be done for the one who believes.”
Immediately the father of the child cried out, “I believe; help my unbelief!”

When Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You spirit that keeps this boy from speaking and hearing, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”
After crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, “He is dead.”
But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he was able to stand.
When he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why could we not cast it out?”
He said to them, “This kind can come out only through prayer.”
They went on from there and passed through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know it;
for he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, “The Son of Man is to be betrayed into human hands, and they will kill him, and three days after being killed, he will rise again.”
But they did not understand what he was saying and were afraid to ask him.
Then they came to Capernaum; and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing about on the way?”
But they were silent, for on the way they had argued with one another who was the greatest.
He sat down, called the twelve, and said to them, “Whoever wants to be first must be last of all and servant of all.”
Then he took a little child and put it among them; and taking it in his arms, he said to them,
“Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes not me but the one who sent me.”
John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he was not following us.”
But Jesus said, “Do not stop him; for no one who does a deed of power in my name will be able soon afterward to speak evil of me.
Whoever is not against us is for us.
For truly I tell you, whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you bear the name of Christ will by no means lose the reward.
“If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones who believe in me, it would be better for you if a great millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea.
If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life maimed than to have two hands and to go to hell, to the unquenchable fire.
And if your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and to be thrown into hell.
And if your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out; it is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and to be thrown into hell,
where their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.
“For everyone will be salted with fire.
Salt is good; but if salt has lost its saltiness, how can you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”

text.NRSV: He left that place and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan. And crowds again gathered around him; and, as was his custom, he again taught them.
text.Rotherham: And, from thence, arising, he cometh into the bounds of Judaea and beyond the Jordan,—and there come together again, multitudes unto him, and, as he had been wont, again, was he teaching them.

text.NRSV: Some Pharisees came, and to test him they asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
text.Rotherham: And Pharisees coming near were questioning him—whether it is allowed a husband to divorce a wife, testing him.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “What did Moses command you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—What unto you did, Moses, command?

text.NRSV: They said, “Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Moses permitted, to write, a roll of dismissal, and to divorce.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “Because of your hardness of heart he wrote this commandment for you.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—In view of your hardness of heart, wrote he for you this commandment;

text.NRSV: But from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.’
text.Rotherham: But, from the beginning of creation, male and female, made he [them];

text.NRSV: ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, shall a man leave behind his father and mother,

text.NRSV: and the two shall become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two, but one flesh.
text.Rotherham: and, the two, shall become, one flesh; so that, no longer, are they two, but, one flesh.

text.NRSV: Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
text.Rotherham: What then, God, hath yoked together, let, a man, not put asunder.

text.NRSV: Then in the house the disciples asked him again about this matter.
text.Rotherham: And, [coming] into the house again, the disciples, concerning this, were questioning him;

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;
text.Rotherham: and he saith unto them—Whosoever shall divorce his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her;

text.NRSV: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
text.Rotherham: And, if, she, divorcing her husband, marry another, she committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: People were bringing little children to him in order that he might touch them; and the disciples spoke sternly to them.
text.Rotherham: And they were bringing unto him children, that he might, touch, them,—the disciples, however, were rebuking them.

text.NRSV: But when Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the little children come to me; do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, seeing it, was greatly displeased, and said unto them—Suffer the children to come unto me,—do not hinder them; for, of such, is the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it.”
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—Whosoever shall not welcome the kingdom of God, as a child, in nowise shall enter thereinto.

text.NRSV: And he took them up in his arms, laid his hands on them, and blessed them.
text.Rotherham: And, folding them in his arms, he was blessing them, having laid his hands upon them.

text.NRSV: As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up and knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And, as he was going forth into a road, one, running, and kneeling before him, was questioning him—Good Teacher! what shall I do that, life age—abiding, I may inherit?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Why callest thou me, good? None, is good, save one—God.

text.NRSV: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; You shall not defraud; Honor your father and mother.’”
text.Rotherham: The commandments, thou knowest,—Do not commit murder, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal; Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honour thy father and mother.

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Teacher, I have kept all these since my youth.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—Teacher! all these things, have I kept, from my youth.

text.NRSV: Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said, “You lack one thing; go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said unto him—One thing, unto thee, is wanting;—Withdraw! whatsoever thou hast, sell, and give unto [the] destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come! be following me.

text.NRSV: When he heard this, he was shocked and went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
text.Rotherham: And, he, becoming gloomy because of the word, departed sorrowing, for he was holding, many possessions.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How hard it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
text.Rotherham: And, looking around, Jesus saith unto his disciples—With what difficulty, shall, they who have money, enter, into the kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: And the disciples were perplexed at these words. But Jesus said to them again, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
text.Rotherham: And, the disciples, were in amazement, because of the words. But, Jesus, again answering, saith unto them—Children! how difficult, it is, to enter, into the kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: It is, easier, for a camel, through the eye of a needle, to pass, than for, a rich man, into the kingdom of God, to enter.

text.NRSV: They were greatly astounded and said to one another, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And, they, were being exceedingly struck with astonishment, saying unto him—Who, then, can, be saved?

text.NRSV: Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but not for God; for God all things are possible.”
text.Rotherham: Looking at them, Jesus saith—With men, impossible, but not, with God; for, all things, are possible, with God

text.NRSV: Peter began to say to him, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”
text.Rotherham: Peter began to be saying unto him—Lo! we, have left all, and followed thee!

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields, for my sake and for the sake of the good news,
text.Rotherham: Jesus said—Verily, I say unto you—There is, no one, who hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or mother, or father, or children, or lands, for the sake of me, and [for the sake of] the glad-message,

text.NRSV: who will not receive a hundredfold now in this age—houses, brothers and sisters, mothers and children, and fields, with persecutions—and in the age to come eternal life.
text.Rotherham: who shall not receive a hundredfold, now, in this season, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands,—with persecutions, and, in the age that is coming, life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.”
text.Rotherham: But, many, shall be—first-last, and [the] Last-first.

text.NRSV: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them; they were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. He took the twelve aside again and began to tell them what was to happen to him,
text.Rotherham: Now they were in the way, going up unto Jerusalem, and Jesus was going on before them,—and they were in amazement; and, they who followed, were in fear. And, taking unto himself, again, the twelve, he began to be saying unto them, as to the things about to befall him—

text.NRSV: saying, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death; then they will hand him over to the Gentiles;
text.Rotherham: Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem; and, the Son of Man, will be delivered up, unto the Chief-priests and the Scribes,—and they will condemn him to death, and deliver him up unto the nations;

text.NRSV: they will mock him, and spit upon him, and flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: And they will mock him, and spit upon him, and scourge him, and, slay;—and, after three days, will he, arise.

text.NRSV: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came forward to him and said to him, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”
text.Rotherham: And there come near unto him, James and John, the [two] sons of Zebedee,—saying unto him—Teacher! we desire, that, whatsoever we shall ask thee, thou wilt do for us.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “What is it you want me to do for you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—What desire ye, I should do for you?

text.NRSV: And they said to him, “Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—Grant us, that, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left, we may sit, in thy glory.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to them, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said unto them—Ye know not what ye are asking: Are ye able to drink the cup that, I, am to drink? or to be immersed, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed?

text.NRSV: They replied, “We are able.” Then Jesus said to them, “The cup that I drink you will drink; and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized;
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—We are able. And, Jesus, said unto them—The cup which, I, am to drink, ye shall drink, and, with the immersion wherewith, I, am to be immersed, shall ye be immersed;

text.NRSV: but to sit at my right hand or at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
text.Rotherham: But, to sit on my right hand and on my left, is not mine to give, except unto them for whom it hath been prepared.

text.NRSV: When the ten heard this, they began to be angry with James and John.
text.Rotherham: And the ten, hearing, began to be sorely displeased, concerning James and John.

text.NRSV: So Jesus called them and said to them, “You know that among the Gentiles those whom they recognize as their rulers lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, calling them near, saith unto them—Ye know that, they who think to rule the nations, lord it over them, and, their great ones, wield authority over them;

text.NRSV: But it is not so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you must be your servant,
text.Rotherham: But, not so, it is, among you. But, whosoever desireth to become, great, among you, shall be, your minister,

text.NRSV: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be slave of all.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever desireth, among you, to be, first, shall be, servant of all;

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
text.Rotherham: For, even the Son of Man, came not to be ministered unto, but minister,—and to give his life, a ransom instead of many.

text.NRSV: They came to Jericho. As he and his disciples and a large crowd were leaving Jericho, Bartimaeus son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the roadside.
text.Rotherham: And they come into Jericho. And, as he was journeying forth from Jericho, and his disciples, and a considerable multitude, the son of Timaeus, blind Bar-Timaeus, a beggar, was sitting beside the road.

text.NRSV: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout out and say, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing that it was, Jesus the Nazarene, he began to be crying aloud, and saying—O Son of David! Jesus! have mercy on me.

text.NRSV: Many sternly ordered him to be quiet, but he cried out even more loudly, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And many were rebuking him, that he might hold his peace; but, he, so much the more, was crying aloud, O Son of David, have mercy on me.

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and said, “Call him here.” And they called the blind man, saying to him, “Take heart; get up, he is calling you.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming to a stand, Jesus said—Call him! And they call the blind man, saying unto him—Take courage! rise! he calleth thee!

text.NRSV: So throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, he, throwing off his mantle—springing to his feet, came unto Jesus.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man said to him, “My teacher, let me see again.”
text.Rotherham: And, answering him, Jesus said—What desirest thou, I should do for thee? And, the blind man, said unto him—Rabboni! that may recover sight.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Go; your faith has made you well.” Immediately he regained his sight and followed him on the way.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Go thy way! thy faith, hath saved thee. And, straightway, he recovered sight, and was following him in the road.

verses.text: When they were approaching Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
and said to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden; untie it and bring it.
If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here immediately.’”
They went away and found a colt tied near a door, outside in the street. As they were untying it,
some of the bystanders said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
They told them what Jesus had said; and they allowed them to take it.
Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it; and he sat on it.
Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut in the fields.
Then those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
Blessed is the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
Then he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see whether perhaps he would find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.

Then they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling and those who were buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves;
and he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
He was teaching them and saying, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it a den of robbers.”
And when the chief priests and the scribes heard it, they kept looking for a way to kill him; for they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was spellbound by his teaching.
And when evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
Then Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
Truly I tell you, if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and do not doubt in your heart, but believe that what you say will come to pass, it will be done for you.
So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
“Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.”
Again they came to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him
and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them?”
Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin? Answer me.”
They argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
But shall we say, ‘Of human origin’?”—they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as actually a prophet.
So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

text.NRSV: Then he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the wine press, and built a watchtower; then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
text.Rotherham: And he began to be speaking unto them, in parables,—A man planted, a vineyard, and put round it a wall, and digged a wine-vat, and built a tower,—and let it out to husbandmen; and left home.

text.NRSV: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his share of the produce of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth, unto the husbandmen, in the season, a servant, that, from the husbandmen, he might receive of the fruits of the vineyard;

text.NRSV: But they seized him, and beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: and, taking him, they beat him, and sent him away, empty.

text.NRSV: And again he sent another slave to them; this one they beat over the head and insulted.
text.Rotherham: And, again, sent he forth unto them, another servant; and, him, they smote on the head, and dishonoured.

text.NRSV: Then he sent another, and that one they killed. And so it was with many others; some they beat, and others they killed.
text.Rotherham: And another sent he forth; and, him, they slew. And many others; some, indeed beating, and, others, slaying.

text.NRSV: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
text.Rotherham: Yet one, had he, a son beloved: he sent him forth last unto them, saying—They will pay deference unto my son!

text.NRSV: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
text.Rotherham: But, those husbandmen, unto themselves, said—This, is the heir: Come! let us slay him, and, ours, shall be, the inheritance.

text.NRSV: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And, taking, they slew him, and cast him forth outside the vineyard.

text.NRSV: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
text.Rotherham: What will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come, and destroy the husbandmen, and let the vineyard unto others.

text.NRSV: Have you not read this scripture: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
text.Rotherham: Have ye not, this scripture, read—A stone which the builders rejected, the same, hath become head of the corner!

text.NRSV: this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?”
text.Rotherham: From the Lord, hath this come to pass, and is marvellous in our eyes?

text.NRSV: When they realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowd. So they left him and went away.
text.Rotherham: And they were seeking, to secure, him,—and were in fear of the multitude; for they perceived that, against them, the parable he had spoken. And, leaving him, they departed.

text.NRSV: Then they sent to him some Pharisees and some Herodians to trap him in what he said.
text.Rotherham: And they send forth unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, that they might, catch, him, in discourse.

text.NRSV: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?
text.Rotherham: And, coming, they say unto him—Teacher! we know that, true, thou art, and it concerneth thee not about anyone,—for thou lookest not unto the face of men; but, in truth, the way of God, dost teach:—Is it allowable to give tax unto Caesar, or not? Should we give, or should we not give?

text.NRSV: Should we pay them, or should we not?” But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why are you putting me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me see it.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them—Why are ye, tempting, me? Bring me a denary, that I may see it.

text.NRSV: And they brought one. Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?” They answered, “The emperor’s.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, brought one. And he saith unto them—Whose, is this image, and the inscription? And, they, said unto him—Caesar’s.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said—the things of Caesar, render, unto Caesar, and, the things of God, unto God. And they were marvelling at him.

text.NRSV: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and asked him a question, saying,
text.Rotherham: And there come Sadducees unto him,—who, indeed say—Resurrection, there is none! and they were questioning him, saying—

text.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
text.Rotherham: Teacher! Moses, wrote for us, that—If one’s brother die, and leave behind a wife, and leave no child, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

text.NRSV: There were seven brothers; the first married and died childless;
text.Rotherham: Seven brethren, there were: and, the first, took a wife, and, dying, left no seed,—

text.NRSV: and the second married her and died, leaving no child; and the third likewise;
text.Rotherham: And, the second, took her, and died, not leaving behind seed,—and, the third, likewise,—

text.NRSV: none of the seven left a child. Last of all the woman herself died.
text.Rotherham: And, the seven, left no seed; last of all, the woman also, died:—

text.NRSV: In the resurrection whose wife will she be? For the seven had married her.”
text.Rotherham: In the resurrection,—of, which, of them shall she be, wife? For, the seven, had her to wife.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Is not this the reason you are wrong, that you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God?
text.Rotherham: Jesus said unto them—Are ye not, for this cause, deceiving yourselves, knowing neither the Scriptures, nor the power of God?

text.NRSV: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
text.Rotherham: For, when, from among the dead, they rise, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like messengers in the heavens.

text.NRSV: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story about the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the dead, that they, do rise,—Have ye not read in the book of Moses, at the Bush, how God spake unto him, saying—I [am] the God of Abraham, and God of Isaac, and God of Jacob:—

text.NRSV: He is God not of the dead, but of the living; you are quite wrong.”
text.Rotherham: He is not a God of, dead, men, but of, living. Greatly, are ye deceiving yourselves.

text.NRSV: One of the scribes came near and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, he asked him, “Which commandment is the first of all?”
text.Rotherham: And one of the Scribes, coming near, hearing them discussing, seeing that, well, he had answered them, began to question him—Which is the chief commandment of all?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “The first is, ‘Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one;
text.Rotherham: Jesus answered—The chief is: Hear! O Israel,—The Lord our God, is, one Lord;

text.NRSV: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou love the Lord thy God, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,—and with all thy mind; and with all thy strength.

text.NRSV: The second is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
text.Rotherham: The second, is, this—Thou shalt love, thy neighbour, as thyself. Greater than these, other commandment, is there none.

text.NRSV: Then the scribe said to him, “You are right, Teacher; you have truly said that ‘he is one, and besides him there is no other’;
text.Rotherham: The Scribe said unto him—Well, Teacher! in truth, hast thou said—He is, One, and there is none other, than he;

text.NRSV: and ‘to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the strength,’ and ‘to love one’s neighbor as oneself,’—this is much more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
text.Rotherham: And, to love him, with all the heart, and with all the understanding,—and with all the might; and to love one’s neighbour as one’s self, is, abundantly more, than all the whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.

text.NRSV: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” After that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, seeing him, that, with intelligence he answered, said unto him—Not far, [art thou] from the kingdom of God! And, no one, any longer, was daring to, question, him.

text.NRSV: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?
